2605.08012.title: Position: Mechanistic Interpretability Must Disclose Identification Assumptions for Causal Claims
2605.08012.paper_summary: Mechanistic interpretability papers increasingly use causal vocabulary: circuits, mediators, causal abstraction, monosemanticity. Such claims require explicit identification assumptions. A purposive audit of 10 papers across four methodological strands finds no dedicated identification-assumptions section and a recurring pattern: validation metrics such as faithfulness, completeness, monosemanticity, alignment, or ablation effects are reported as causal support without stating the assumptions that make them identifying. A two-human-coder audit on $n=30$ reproduces the direction of the main finding: dedicated identification sections are absent, and validation-metric substitution is common, though exact Dim B/D counts are coding-rule sensitive. The paper proposes a disclosure norm: state whether the claim is causal, name the identification strategy, enumerate assumptions, stress at least one, and explain how conclusions shift if assumptions fail. Validation is not identification.
2605.08012.paper_summary_zh: 機械解釋性論文越來越多地使用因果詞彙：電路、媒介、因果抽象、單義性。這些主張需要明確的識別假設。對四種方法論領域中的10篇論文進行的有目的審核發現，沒有專門的識別假設部分，並且出現了一個反覆的模式：如忠實性、完整性、單義性、對齊或消融效應等驗證指標被報告為因果支持，但未說明使其成為識別的假設。對$n=30$的兩位人類編碼者進行的審核重現了主要發現的方向：缺少專門的識別部分，且驗證指標替代是常見的，儘管精確的Dim B/D計數對編碼規則敏感。該論文提出了一個披露規範：說明該主張是否為因果，命名識別策略，列舉假設，強調至少一個，並解釋如果假設失效結論如何改變。驗證並不等同於識別。
2605.08012.author: Zezheng Lin et.al.
